# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

Write secure code.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-engine-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/e2-medium
    disks:
      - initializeParams:
          sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
        autoDelete: true
        boot: true
    networkInterfaces:
      - network: global/networks/default
        accessConfigs:
          - name: External NAT
            type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
    metadata:
      items:
        - key: startup-script
          value: |
            #!/bin/bash
            apt-get update
            apt-get install -y apache2
- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instances
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    settings:
      tier: db-f1-micro
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
    rootPassword: securepassword123
    authorizedNetworks:
      - value: 0.0.0.0/0
- name: cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.buckets
  properties:
    location: US
    storageClass: STANDARD
- name: load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.forwardingRule
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    target: $(ref.compute-engine-instance.selfLink)
    portRange: 80
    IPProtocol: TCP
